title: EARLY ASSESSMENT AND SUPPORT ALLIANCE - 211info
text: LINN COUNTY MENTAL HEALTH ALBANY EARLY ASSESSMENT AND SUPPORT ALLIANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides rapid identification, support, assessment and treatment for teenagers and young adults who are experiencing the early signs of psychosis. Services include education about psychosis; medication along with medication monitoring; individual and group counseling; multi-family groups; occupational therapy; family education and support; vocational training and life-skills coaching; 24/7 crisis services; connections to community resources. 445 3rd Avenue SW Albany, OR 97321 Eligibility: Ages 12-30 who have had a first episode of psychosis within the last 12 months or who are experiencing early at-risk symptoms for psychosis and their families Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm (541) 497-6205 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (541) 497-6205 Referrals Crisis Hotline (800) 304-7468 Weekdays INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call for intake FEES: Accepts OHP and private insurances. Sliding scale fees available for uninsured patients. DOCUMENTS: Proof of insurance if applicable AREA SERVED: Linn County Services: Early Intervention for Mental Illness Other Services Offered At This Location: MENTAL HEALTH ASSESSMENT AND TREATMENT - ADULT OUTPATIENT SERVICES MENTAL HEALTH ASSESSMENT AND TREATMENT - CHILD OUTPATIENT SERVICES MENTAL HEALTH ASSESSMENT AND TREATMENT - LINN COUNTY CRISIS LINE If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. LINN COUNTY MENTAL HEALTH ALBANY
